note: Washington expanded Medicaid (Apple Health). Adults up to 138% FPL qualify. Apply through Washington Connection.

programs.name: SNAP (Washington)
programs.description: Washington administers federal SNAP (called Basic Food in WA). Apply through Washington Connection.

application.where: washingtonconnection.org or local DSHS Community Services Office

programs.name: Apple Health (Washington Medicaid)
programs.description: Washington's Apple Health program covers adults up to 138% FPL and children up to 312% FPL. No premium for most enrollees. Comprehensive coverage including dental.

monthly_benefit_range.note: No premium for most enrollees. Washington has one of the most comprehensive Medicaid programs.

income.note: Washington expanded Medicaid — adults up to 138% FPL qualify through Apple Health.

programs.name: WorkFirst (Washington TANF)
programs.description: Washington's WorkFirst program provides monthly cash to families with children. One of the more generous TANF programs — a family of 3 can receive up to approximately $658/month.

monthly_benefit_range.note: Approximately $658/month for family of 3 — among the higher TANF amounts in the US.

income.note: Must be below income limits; WorkFirst participation plan required

application.where: washingtonconnection.org or local DSHS Community Services Office
